section_summary: 48 entries triaged. 34 LEAVE / 7 ARCHIVE-or-REFRESH / 0 ANGEL_OF_DEATH / 7 REFRESH. Two CRITICAL findings: (1) two index lines point to NONEXISTENT files (feedback_margin_not_tax.md line 80, feedback_open_chapters_compilations.md line 90). (2) feedback_named_entity_canon_search_reflex.md exists but has NO Feedback-section index line — it's referenced from Project Memories line 161 as a discoverability path, leaving Bishop's behavior-rule index incomplete. (3) project_the_cathedral_effect.md description (line 119) is stale: claims 'Untested' but B131 5-condition cluster validated it. (4) feedback_build_for_long_haul.md (line 94) is SUPERSEDED by feedback_long_haul_and_fix_along_the_way.md per the latter file's own self-claim — should ARCHIVE the index line. (5) Section ordering: B132 rules at top correctly, but feedback_keep_analysis_visible (line 106) and feedback_aa_formal_paired_with_memory_entry (line 107) are buried below the 200-line MEMORY.md truncation cutoff and should be promoted into the top 10 because they're load-bearing every session.
top_section_ordering_recommendation: Top 7 rules to sit before line 200 cutoff (in priority order): (1) feedback_librarian_consult_first_always.md — MANDATORY first tool, hook-enforced; (2) feedback_codecopy_number_ask_second.md — MANDATORY second action, B132 install; (3) feedback_empirically_valid_praise_only.md — B132 Founder-mandatory, default-neutral; (4) feedback_one_new_primitive_per_session.md — B132 architectural-debt control; (5) feedback_keep_analysis_visible.md — Bishop is thinker-coordinator (PROMOTE from 106); (6) feedback_aa_formal_paired_with_memory_entry.md — filing-time atomic coupling, BRIDLE Rule 13 candidate (PROMOTE from 107); (7) feedback_session_closeout_updates_librarian.md — closeout invariant with pre-close audit (PROMOTE from 115). Rationale: these 7 are the rules whose violation costs the MOST per session in either Founder time or substrate drift. The 200-line truncation cutoff means everything below ~line 200 may not load — these 7 MUST be inside the load window.
cross_section_concerns: 15 project_*.md entries are interleaved within the Feedback section instead of living in the Project Memories section (line 122+). This is structural drift not strictly in feedback-rules subagent scope but documented for cross-section coordination. Recommendation: identity-canonical or a follow-up sweeper pass should re-section these to Project Memories, leaving Feedback section purely behavioral rules. This would naturally compact the Feedback section and bring the load-bearing rules within the 200-line cutoff window.
